formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Allometric Growth",
    "formula": "Isometry: (M1/M2) = (L1/L2)^3, 
                  Allometry: (M1/M2) = k(L1/L2)^n",
    "variables": "M1: mass of organ 1, M2: mass of organ 2, 
                   L1: length of organ 1, L2: length of organ 2, 
                   k: constant, n: allometric constant",
    "units": "mass: kg, length: m",
    "notes": "Used to study the relationship between organ size and body size"
  },
  {
    "name": "Huxley's Law",
    "formula": "M = kL^a",
    "variables": "M: mass, L: length, k: constant, a: exponent",
    "units": "mass: kg, length: m",
    "notes": "Used to describe the relationship between organ size and body size"
  },
  {
    "name": "Allen's Rule",
    "formula": "Blood vessels in extremities are smaller in cold-blooded animals than in warm-blooded animals",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to explain the adaptation of blood vessels to maintain body temperature"
  },
  {
    "name": "Bergmann's Rule",
    "formula": "Body mass increases with geographical latitude",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to explain the adaptation of body size to environmental temperature"
  },
  {
    "name": "Gross Body Mass",
    "formula": "Total mass of an animal",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "kg",
    "notes": "Used to describe the total mass of an animal"
  },
  {
    "name": "Skeletal System",
    "formula": "Endoskeleton: internal skeleton, Exoskeleton: external skeleton",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to describe the types of skeletal systems found in animals"
  },
  {
    "name": "Muscular System",
    "formula": "Skeletal muscles are attached to bones via tendons",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to describe the structure and function of the muscular system"
  },
  {
    "name": "Nervous System",
    "formula": "Reflex arc: sensory neuron → integration center → motor neuron",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to describe the basic structure and function of the nervous system"
  },
  {
    "name": "Circulatory System",
    "formula": "Double circulation: oxygenated blood from heart to lungs, deoxygenated blood from heart to body",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to describe the basic structure and function of the circulatory system"
  },
  {
    "name": "Respiratory System",
    "formula": "Inhalation: air enters lungs, Exhalation: air leaves lungs",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to describe the basic structure and function of the respiratory system"
  },
  {
    "name": "Digestive System",
    "formula": "Ingestion: food enters mouth, Digestion: food is broken down in digestive tract, Absorption: nutrients are absorbed into bloodstream",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Used to describe the basic structure and function of the digestive system"
  }
]
```

This formula sheet covers various concepts related to structural organisation in animals, including allometric growth, Huxley's law, Allen's rule, Bergmann's rule, and others.
